slides.title: Building digital ecosystem
slides.subtitle: Building & Living for a leading banking group
slides.context: In view of increasing competition from FinTechs and PropTechs, traditional banks must find ways to maintain the customer interface and develop new revenue potential beyond classic financial services.

sections.title: Strategic target image
details: Conception of a strategic target image for the holistic occupation of different people.
Customer journeys in the 'Building & Living' area and creation of a comprehensive market analysis.
Definition of a balanced portfolio of opportunity areas and initiatives.

sections.title: Customer offer & testing
details: Analysis and prioritization of different 'living worlds'.
Creation and prioritization of a long list of over 130 service ideas for the ecosystem offering.
Rapid prototyping of four services, testing for end customers as well as potential sponsors and partners internally and externally.

sections.title: Implementation roadmap
details: Derived from the strategic target image and the customer's existing assets, an implementation roadmap for 12 months and a high-level business case were defined.
In addition to technical and organizational requirements, technical specifications were also defined for part of the offer.

sections.title: Opinion formation process
details: Regular coordination in direction and decision-making committees as well as continuous collaboration with the core and project team of 15 participants.
Preparation and moderation of workshops, implementation of jour fixes, management of small groups.

slides.title: Development of an ecosystem strategy
slides.context: Traditional banks are coming under increasing pressure in competition with tech players for customer access and interface. In response, we developed an ecosystem strategy as well as new business model and service approaches for a leading German banking group in order to systematically strengthen the customer interface.

sections.title: Strategic target image
details: At the core of the ecosystem strategy, we have four opportunity spaces for dedicated innovation activities identified.
Defined the requirements for a future product and service portfolio.
Developed a strategic framework for the allocation of resources within the opportunity areas.

sections.title: Customer survey
details: Conducted a representative, quantitative customer survey (> 1000 private customers, > 250 corporate customers).
Validated hypotheses for private and corporate customer business.
Derived strategic fields of action for new value creation models and value propositions.

sections.title: Conception sprints
details: Developed product concepts in three innovation fields - new contact points, new services, new business models.
Result: > 70 PK and FK concepts on longlist and 18 prioritized.
Detailed concepts for an implementation roadmap.

sections.title: Prototyping & validation
details: Four particularly successful promising product concepts were transferred to a separate governance structure at predefined project times.
These were prototyped in a sprint logic, validated and, if the result was positive, immediately implemented.

slides.title: Product turnaround for a Germany-wide loyalty program
slides.context: Our customer - a central institution of a banking association - wanted to strategically relaunch its advantage program (with < 9 million participants) and position it as a central loyalty program in the association. TLGG Consulting was commissioned for a product turnaround to make the program more attractive for users, banks, and partners.

sections.title: Strategy & vision development
details: Developed a strategic target and future vision for the program considering end customers as well as market and competitive situation.
Obtained internal support for the new vision through stakeholder discussions.

sections.title: Program further development
details: Redesigned the program according to the strategic target.
Further developed the added value and bonus mechanisms to ensure program relevance for primary banks, customers, and partners.

sections.title: Support at committee meetings
details: Supported preparation for committee meetings with presentation creation and stakeholder discussions.
Helped to confirm the target image in relevant committees through internal networking.

sections.title: Implementation scenarios
details: Supported analysis of organizational and technical settings, time/cost estimates, and alternatives for bringing the program to market.
Advised on evaluation and selection of implementation partners.

slides.title: Platform strategy for a decentralized organization
slides.context: Digital brokerage platforms create transparency in financial products and increasingly occupy the customer interface. A decentralized organization commissioned TLGG Consulting to formulate strategic recommendations for sales.

sections.title: Decision template and business case
details: Created a decision template and strategy paper with strategic recommendations, three business cases, and requirements for further implementation.

sections.title: Prototyping of solution approaches
details: Rapid prototyping of two click dummies and two product mockups as tangible solutions.
Conception, control, and evaluation of a quantitative and qualitative test for validation with internal and external target groups.

sections.title: Coordination with around 100 stakeholders
details: Regular coordination in direction and decision-making committees with a core and project team of 30.
Preparation and moderation of workshops, implementation of jour fixes, and management of small groups.

slides.title: Optimization of the transformation strategy of a large German insurance group
slides.context: Like many large, traditional companies, our customer faced the challenge of becoming fit for digitalization. Despite good framework conditions - such as a CDO with its own unit and 'transformation managers' - there was a lack of a unified vision and a clear target image.

sections.title: Digital strategy
details: Supported with a broad outside-in perspective, resulting in a digital strategy approved by the board, including a digital roadmap.

sections.title: Bespoke organizational form
details: Worked iteratively with the core team to develop and introduce a tailor-made organizational form for the digital unit.

sections.title: Collaborative roadmap process
details: Developed a process to coordinate all digital initiatives and projects, incorporating all perspectives into further development.

sections.title: A strategy for the CDO
details: Developed a strategy in close coordination with the Chief Digital Officer to integrate the digital unit more closely within the group.

slides.title: Digital solutions for a young target group
slides.context: For many people under 30, 'building savings' is a thing of the past. Cheap interest rates and FinTechs have left old providers behind. We aimed to make the product future-proof and explore new business models.

sections.title: Exploration opportunity space
details: Exploratory interviews revealed new opportunities with a changed product and approach.
Market and competition analyses identified gaps in the market for new customer segments.

sections.title: Ideation & prioritization
details: Developed and prioritized ideas in workshops, assessing feasibility, investments, and existing assets.

sections.title: Concept & Decision template
details: Refined prioritized ideas into concepts.
Collected facts in a decision template, including assessment of implementation.

sections.title: Results
details: 15 ideas including a blockchain-based platform for real estate history, crowdfunding for real estate purchases, and an advisor platform.
Provided methodology and know-how for an agile, independent team.

slides.title: New target operating model & product strategy for E-mobility
slides.context: A green electricity provider expanded its e-mobility division by merging with a charging infrastructure provider. We orchestrated the operational and legal activities of the merger and defined a strategic product roadmap.

sections.title: Pre-merger management
details: Coordinated operational and legal tasks before the merger and developed the post-merger roadmap for synergy realization.

sections.title: Validation of product strategy
details: Identified trends in charging applications and consolidated product portfolios focused on recurring revenue, cross-selling, and efficiency improvements.

sections.title: New operating model
details: Identified competency gaps and defined a new Target Operating Model (TOM) with new roles and governance structures.

sections.title: PMI effects
details: Modeled financial scenarios, managed change with a focus on cultural integration, and achieved OPEX savings through centralization, automation, and IT alignment.

slides.title: Growth strategy for a leading medium-sized bank
slides.context: Interest rate pressure and new customer requirements are putting today's corporate banking under pressure. We aimed to develop a 'beyond banking' vision for growth outside the classic core business.

sections.title: Customer understanding build-up
details: Analyzed and prioritized 14 business challenges in relevant customer segments based on outside-in perspective.

sections.title: Derive opportunity spaces
details: Created a long list of five opportunity areas and prioritized three in a joint strategy workshop.

sections.title: Validate and pilot feasibility
details: Validated priority topics based on internal and external expert interviews, focusing on embedded finance and digital CFO ecosystems.

sections.title: Design roadmap
details: Developed an implementation concept, networked with strategic partners, and created roadmaps for pilot projects.

slides.title: Validating and building a D2C brand for hearing aids
slides.context: A leading hearing aid manufacturer sought to enter the market with an innovative product. We focused on addressing new customer segments via digital sales channels.

sections.title: Go-to-market
details: Conducted smoke tests with landing pages, ads, and non-functional online shops to validate go-to-market hypotheses.

sections.title: Digital ecosystem
details: Designed user flows, services, and requirements for the e-commerce ecosystem, selecting store systems and integrations.

sections.title: Operations
details: Selected and onboarded customer service and fulfillment partners, defining core processes for a seamless user experience.

sections.title: Results
details: Validated go-to-market strategy for two products, designed user flows and services, and selected shop systems and partners for three main markets.

slides.title: Digital campaign for addressing young people for the nursing service
slides.context: The Federal Ministry for Family, Women, Senior Citizens and Youth aimed to inspire careers in nursing and attract potential trainees, students, and pupils.

sections.title: Goal-oriented implementation
details: Defined and characterized three target groups and addressed them with the slogan 'Care can do something.'
Used a diverse cast and distinctive design, including a specially developed logo.
Implemented a 360° campaign across online and offline channels.

sections.title: Results
details: Reached over 129 million users online (social media) and over 93 million contacts (print & digital).
Achieved an average view-through rate of campaign spots on YouTube over 50%.
Further measures will be designed and implemented up to and including 2025, including additional productions, content formats, and a care award.
